title: Intense Ethiopian Chickpeas Stew
description: A bitter, aerated, comforting stew featuring double-fried chickpea balls and melted emmentaler wrapped in fragrant pandan leaf.
instructions: Heat 20 g of niter kibbeh in a pot over medium heat until fragrant.
Add 50 g diced onion, 5 g minced garlic, and 5 g minced ginger and sauté until translucent.
Stir in 30 g tomato paste and 10 g berbere spice mix, cooking until the paste darkens slightly.
Pour in 150 ml coconut milk, bring to a simmer, and add 200 g cooked chickpeas, stirring to coat.
Reduce heat and let the stew thicken for 10 minutes, then fold in 50 g grated emmentaler and stir until melted.
While the stew simmers, prepare a thin batter of chickpeas, onion, and spices, coat the small balls and double-fry them until crisp.
Once the stew reaches desired thickness, spoon it into a prepared pandan leaf, add a few fried chickpea balls, and fold the leaf into a wrap.
Serve the wrapped stew immediately, accompanied by a small dollop of aerated coconut foam made by whipping 30 g coconut cream with a pinch of sugar.
Optionally sprinkle fresh herbs before serving.
